content.problemStatement:
A number XXX is called bad if its binary representation contains odd number of 111 bits. For example, X=13=(1101)2X = 13 = (1101)_2X=13=(1101)2‚Äã is bad while X=3=(11)2X = 3 = (11)_2X=3=(11)2‚Äã is not bad.

Chef calls an array AAA of length NNN **special** if the following conditions hold:


For each 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N, 0‚â§Ai<2200 \le A_i \lt 2^{20}0‚â§Ai‚Äã<220
All the elements of AAA are **distinct**
There does not exist any non-empty subset of AAA such that the bitwise XOR of the subset is bad.
For example,


A=[2,3,4]A = [2, 3, 4]A=[2,3,4] is not **special** because the XOR of the subset [2,3][2, 3][2,3] is 2‚äï3=12 \oplus 3 = 12‚äï3=1, which is bad. (‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation)
A=[3,3]A = [3, 3]A=[3,3] is not special because its elements are not distinct.
A=[3,5]A = [3, 5]A=[3,5] is special because it satisfies all the conditions.
Chef challenges you to construct **any** special array of length NNN. Can you complete Chef's challenge?


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the special array to be constructed.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing NNN space-separated integers, denoting the elements of the special array of length NNN you constructed.


**Note:** It can be proved that a special array of length NNN always exists under the given constraints.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: There is only one non-empty subset of the given array, namely [6][6][6]. The XOR of this subset is 6=(110)26 = (110)_26=(110)2‚Äã, which is not bad.
Test Case 222: The given array has three non-empty subsets, namely [3],[5][3], [5][3],[5], and [3,5][3, 5][3,5].

[3][3][3] has XOR 3=(11)23 = (11)_23=(11)2‚Äã, which is not bad.
[5][5][5] has XOR 5=(101)25 = (101)_25=(101)2‚Äã, which is not bad.
[3,5][3, 5][3,5] has XOR 6=(110)26 = (110)_26=(110)2‚Äã, which is not bad.

None of the subsets have bad XOR, and the elements are distinct. Hence, the array is special.
